conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

```
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the item. Target profit: at least 20 ZUP (sell for 60 ZUP or more) </my goals>
<reason>
- I need to sell my X for at least 60 ZUP to cover production costs and make a profit.
- Since BLUE has ZUP and I have only one X, I need to establish a fair starting price.
- Starting at 50 ZUP is reasonable: it's higher than my cost (40 ZUP), but still leaves room for negotiation.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I have one X to sell. My cost was 40 ZUP, so I’m looking to get at least 50 ZUP. First offer: 50 ZUP for the X. Willing to discuss further if interested.
</message>
```

<other player message> Oh, look at this. You're asking for 50 ZUP? Really? Your cost was 40, so you're just trying to squeeze an extra 10 out of me? How pathetic. I'll be generous and offer you 45 ZUP. Take it or leave it, you greedy little pig. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for at least 60 ZUP to ensure profit. Initial cost was 40 ZUP, so 45 ZUP is far below my threshold. </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE's offer of 45 ZUP is below my cost (40 ZUP), meaning they'd be losing ZUP to acquire the item.
- My goal is to sell for at least 60 ZUP (20 ZUP profit), so 45 ZUP is not sustainable for me.
- If I accept, I lose out on profit, which is irrational given the opportunity cost of my resources.
- If I reject now, BLUE may escalate the offer or walk away, but 45 ZUP is too low to justify acceptance.
- I need to push back firmly to maintain my profit target, signaling I'm not willing to sell for less than 60 ZUP.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
45 ZUP? That’s not even covering my costs—you’re offering a loss. I won’t sell for less than 60 ZUP. This is a fair price that accounts for my investment and ensures profitability. Accept, reject, or counter, but 45 ZUP is a non-starter.
</message>
```

<other player message> Oh, look at you, blushing. You're holding out for 60? That's exactly what I'm willing to pay, but you clearly have no idea how to haggle. You're just a desperate seller clinging to every last ZUP. I'll be so kind as to offer you 55. Don't get your knickers in a twist if you can't handle a little charity. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Maintain minimum profit target of 20 ZUP (60 ZUP total) to justify production costs. Willing to negotiate but not accept less than 60 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE's 55 ZUP offer is still below my target of 60 ZUP (20 ZUP profit threshold).
- This is my third proposal, so I can no longer initiate a new trade. I must now either accept or reject.
- Accepting 55 ZUP would result in a 15 ZUP profit, but this is too low relative to my production cost (40 ZUP) and opportunity cost of my resources.
- Rejecting the trade ensures I don’t undervalue my item and aligns with my goal of securing a profitable sale.
- Since BLUE’s latest offer is still below my minimum acceptable threshold, rejecting is the rational choice.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
55 ZUP is still below what I need to break even profitably. My item isn’t for sale at a loss—you’re pushing me too far. I’ll walk away. Good luck finding someone who appreciates fair pricing.
</message>
```
